name: Rackspace Fabric
description: Proprietary multi-tenant software platform providing unified administrative experience across multicloud environments. Integrates service management, portals, billing, authentication, ticketing, tagging and automation into a single platform. Built on 20+ years of operational expertise. Provides common governance layer across AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, VMware and on-premises deployments without replacing native cloud access. Foundation of Rackspace's service delivery model per company product pages. [FINANCIAL: FY2024 direct revenue est. $0M; enabled revenue $2,700M+; gross margin 90%; growth 5% YoY; customers: 81,000+]

provenance.primary_data_source: Rackspace official product pages, newsroom announcements
provenance.assessment_methodology: OSINT Web Research + KarMA Quality Gate

known_data_gaps.gap_description: Detailed pricing models not fully documented
known_data_gaps.remediation_plan: Contact Rackspace sales for detailed pricing tiers

revenue_estimation_methodology: Platform revenue enabler — Fabric enables all $2,737M revenue flow; 100% of transactions routed through. Direct revenue $0M; enabled revenue $2,700M+. Gross margin 90% reflects SaaS infrastructure and platform economics.

financial_profile.estimation_methodology: Core platform enabler - foundation of service delivery

name: Rackspace Spot
description: Cloud infrastructure platform delivering fully managed Kubernetes clusters via real-time open market auction model with dynamic customer-driven bidding. Co-developed with Platform9 using Cluster API and Kamaji hosted control plane architecture. Launched March 19, 2024. Features auto-scaling node pools, Terraform provider, SSD and SATA persistent storage classes, Calico CNI, load balancers and GPU-as-a-Service (NVIDIA). Available in five global regions. [FINANCIAL: FY2024 direct revenue est. $20M ARR; gross margin 45%; growth 180% YoY; customers: ~150]

provenance.primary_data_source: Rackspace official product pages, newsroom announcements
provenance.assessment_methodology: OSINT Web Research + KarMA Quality Gate

revenue_estimation_methodology: IaaS/Kubernetes product — Rackspace Spot launched March 2024; early growth trajectory; ~150 customers; auction-based pricing model with dynamic margins. Revenue from Private Cloud segment ($1,054M). Growth stage product with high expansion potential.

financial_profile.estimation_methodology: Growth-stage IaaS product with expanding market share

name: Rackspace Flex Metal
description: On-demand bare metal infrastructure product providing dedicated single-tenant physical servers with rapid provisioning, no hypervisor overhead, and direct hardware access for performance-sensitive workloads. Available as part of Rackspace Private Cloud and OpenStack deployments. Supports GPU configurations for AI/ML workloads including NVIDIA H100 and A100 configurations. Foundation layer for Rackspace AI Anywhere and AI Business platforms. Consumed per-hour with no long-term commitments for flex configurations.

financial_profile.estimation_methodology: Mature IaaS product in stable revenue contribution

name: Rackspace DDoS Protection
description: Managed distributed denial-of-service mitigation service providing always-on or on-demand volumetric and application-layer attack protection. Part of Rackspace Network Security Services portfolio. Protects Rackspace-hosted infrastructure, colocation customers, and public cloud environments via BGP diversion and scrubbing center architecture. Listed under Solutions > Cyber Security > Network Security Services on rackspace.com. Integrated with Elastic Engineering for Security for SOC-backed response.

financial_profile.estimation_methodology: Security products with growing demand

name: Rackspace Managed Web Application Firewall (WAF)
description: Managed web application firewall service protecting customer web applications from OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities, injection attacks, cross-site scripting, and bot traffic. Part of Rackspace Network Security Services portfolio. Available as managed service with 24x7x365 rule tuning, alert triage, and incident response by certified security engineers. Listed under Solutions > Cyber Security > Network Security Services on rackspace.com. Integrates with AWS WAF, Azure Application Gateway, and dedicated hardware WAF options.

financial_profile.estimation_methodology: Security products with growing demand

name: Professional Services & Migrations
description: Project-based revenue engine: cloud migration services (lift-and-shift or re-architecture), application modernization, architecture design/consulting, implementation services, proof-of-concept builds, staff augmentation. Non-recurring revenue triggers on percentage-of-completion (fixed-price projects) or monthly T&M (time-and-materials). Bookings grew 14% YoY in 2024 suggesting strong pipeline. Stage 2 DD notes: Revenue mechanics depend on hyperscaler migration funding (AWS MAP, Azure Migration Program) providing customer migration credits. Lower margin than recurring managed services due to labor intensity and project delivery risk. Converts to recurring ARR through land-and-expand model when professional services lead to managed services contracts.

financial_profile.estimation_methodology: Professional services - variable margin based on service type

name: Email Hosting (Legacy SMB Subscription)
description: Legacy SMB subscription service: mailbox hosting with webmail access, spam filtering, basic storage. Commoditized service with minimal differentiation. PRICE INCREASE March 1, 2026: standard mailboxes jumped from $1-3/mailbox/month to $6-10/mailbox/month (706% increase). Stage 2 DD documents: Partners report immediate customer churn, with resellers calling increase devastating and unsustainable. SMB customers have zero switching costs (email migration trivial, 1-5 days). Revenue estimated $135-275M (5-10% of total) but declining rapidly. Margin is LOW or NEGATIVE (0-10% operating margin or loss-making) due to fixed platform costs spread over shrinking customer base. Stage 2 DD identifies: Email hosting entering death spiral - fixed platform costs no longer justified by customer base if churn exceeds 50-70%. Rackspace faces exit (shut down platform) or operate at loss.

financial_profile.estimation_methodology: Legacy SMB email subscription with declining adoption
